chunks.text: In today's video, we will learn about Core Web Vitals and why they are important.  Like this video and keep your energy high.  The thing is that your website should run on all devices in iPad, iPhone, Android, Surface Pro 4.  Because we have to give a good experience to all users through our website.  In this video, we will also learn that there is a hidden tool in Chrome called Lighthouse.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And how you can use it to enhance your website.  So write in the comment section below, Sigma Batch OP.  Let's go to the computer screen.  Let's roll the intro.  So guys, I have come to my computer screen.

chunks.text: And now I will make a new folder whose name is Video 6.  Video 6.  And here in Video 6, I will open VS Code as always.  And the thing I am going to tell you today is very important from the aspect of SEO.  First of all, I want to tell you what is SEO?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization.  If you see any website, it has SEO in it.  Let's take the example of Amazon.in.  I opened Amazon.in.  So Amazon.in wants this website to be loaded as soon as possible.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: So that their products can be searched.  Google is a search engine.  Google's responsibility is to show the best pages to the users.  So Google wants to show only those pages that are fast.  They present the information very well.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And along with that, there are some things in them that the user has a good experience.  For example, let's say there are two pages.  One page has told about dog food.  The other page has also told exactly the same thing about dog food.  But the page 2 of these two is loaded very fast.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: It loads well.  So Google will give it more preference.  Along with that, there are core web vitals.  Which Google considers to rank two pages.  First of all, it is content.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Content is the most important.  What do you have on one page?  If your content is good, then maybe your website can rank without core web vitals.  But if you are competing with a page that has presented almost similar quality content.  Then your core web vitals will be seen.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And if your core web vitals are good, then you will be given preference.  So the first thing that comes in the core web vitals is your CLS.  You see this video here.  Which I have run from web.dev website.  You will see that someone comes to your website.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And he is trying to cancel the order here.  And by mistake, the time he canceled, this thing above became visible.  And he shifted the layout.  And the order is complete.  But the user wanted the order to be incomplete.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: He wanted to click on no.  In this case, the user experience is very bad.  And as you saw in this video, it should not be like this.  That's why our CLS should be as low as possible.  Now what is the definition of CLS?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Cumulative layout shift means.  How much is shifting while the page is loading.  Assume that you have loaded the page.  And first of all, you get to see this.  It was above first.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: And after that, when this key term part was loaded.  So everything shifted down.  Layout should not shift.  If you put width and height in images.  Then the layout will not shift.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: The next metric we are going to talk about is LCP.  And LCP stands for Largest Contextual Paint.  This means that if the user is viewing a page.  Then the biggest element on it.  For example, the biggest part in this is this image.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: How long is it loading?  Means the whole page will be loaded.  But the biggest looking element.  Which is the most important.  How long is it loading?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: According to web.dev and Google.  A good LCP score should be 2.5 seconds.  Means the biggest element should be loaded in 2.5 seconds.  After this comes the third metric which is FID.  Which stands for First Input Delay.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: First Input Delay means that.  If any user has clicked on any input element.  Like on a button or any type of input.  On a page.  So how long does that input process?

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: For example, if you click on a button.  So how long does that button click browser process?  And according to Google and web.dev.  Your input should start processing in less than 100 milliseconds.  So these were the three core web vitals.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTML
chunks.text: Which are very important.  For your information.  These core web vitals are updated.  But these three are very important.  And you should know.
